title: Psychotria brachypus
intro: Psychotria brachypus, also known as the "Iquitos cat's claw," is a vine native to the Amazon rainforest, belonging to the Rubiaceae family. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which may aid in the treatment of various ailments such as arthritis, digestive issues, and even cancer. The bioactive constituents of Psychotria brachypus include alkaloids, such as oxindole and indole, as well as other compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids. The most common herbal preparations made with Psychotria brachypus are capsules, tablets, and teas, often used to support immune function and reduce inflammation.
